strategic_objective: Assess stability and subtle variability of low cardinality features to understand their potential as brittle shortcut signals.
key_checks: Measure cardinality and value distribution stability of features e23-e32 across multiple data partitions
Evaluate class-conditioned variability and subtle deviations within these near-constant features
Compare dependency concentration profiles to detect if these features cluster tightly with class labels
Test shortcut-like predictive leverage of these features individually and in combination
success_criteria: Identification of consistent low cardinality patterns that persist across partitions
Evidence of subtle but meaningful variation that may mitigate pure shortcut risk
Detection of strong shortcut signals that could induce brittle model behavior
Clear characterization of features that warrant exclusion or special handling
router_constraints: maintain focus on cardinality and shortcut-related checks
